- **Path helpers**: `_pseudo_cl_path()`, `_pseudo_cl_cov_path()`, `_xi_integration_path()`, `_cov_integration_path()` in `claims.smk:114-98`
- **Plotting style**: `from plotting_utils import PAPER_MPLSTYLE` then `plt.style.use(PAPER_MPLSTYLE)`
- **COSEBIS from C_ell**: `cosmo_numba.B_modes.cosebis.COSEBIS.cosebis_from_Cell(ell, Cell_E, Cell_B, theta, cache=True)`
- **COSEBIS from xi**: `sp_validation.cosmo_val.CosmologyValidation.calculate_cosebis()`
- **COSEBIS covariance from xi covariance**: `COSEBIS.cosebis_covariance_from_xipm_covariance(theta, cov_xi_pm)`

### Deleted predecessor

The script existed before as `compare_harmonic_config_cosebis.py` (deleted in commit `dd5451c`). It can be recovered via `git show dd5451c -- "*/compare_harmonic_config_cosebis.py"`. It has useful structure but needs cleanup:
- Remove IPython magic, os.chdir, snakemake_interactive
- Remove fine-binning requirement (ell_min/ell_max cuts)
- Use `snakemake.input.*` consistently
- Use `PAPER_MPLSTYLE` instead of hardcoded path
- Simplify to match current claim patterns

### Key files

- `workflow/rules/claims.smk` -- add the rule here
- `workflow/rules/synthesis.smk` -- add to CLAIM_RULES
- `workflow/scripts/` -- new script goes here
- `workflow/config/` -- methodology spec goes here
- `workflow/config/config.yaml` -- COSEBIS and fiducial parameters
- Pseudo-C_ell files: `code/sp_validation/notebooks/cosmo_val/output/pseudo_cl_{version}_blind=A_powspace_nbins=32.fits`

## Skills

`/snakemake`, `/implementing-code`

## Comments
**2026-02-07 18:38** — Feb 7 update: cosmo_numba W_n(ℓ) validated as accurate (0.2-3% at filter peaks). The mode-dependent E_n offsets (ratios 0.01-1.04) are from 32-bin bandpower sampling underresolving oscillatory W_n(ℓ), not a code bug. In sigma units the harmonic/config E_n differences are small for low modes (n≤5-6) but grow for high modes (n>10). B_n consistent between methods. This is not a blocker for the paper — the cross-validation figure should note the sampling limitation for high modes.
**2026-02-07 19:13** — Bandpower undersampling confirmed experimentally (test_bandpower_sampling.py). Same theory C(ℓ), same W_n — only difference is 32 vs 10k ℓ points. 32-bin E_n ratios degrade mode-dependently: 0.99→0.42 for modes 1-6 at full scale cut. cosmo_numba is correct; the 32-bin Simpson integration can't resolve W_n oscillations for n≥3.

fibers.title: Harmonic-space macro generation lacks leak_corr version filtering
fibers.body:
In `generate_paper_macros.py`, the config-space PTE macro loop (line ~203) correctly filters to `leak_corr` versions:
```python
leak_corr_versions = {k: v for k, v in versions.items() if "leak_corr" in k}
```

But the harmonic-space macro loop (line ~253) iterates over all versions without filtering. If the harmonic evidence JSON ever contains both `SP_v1.4.6` and `SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr`, both would map to the same macro name, causing a LaTeX `\newcommand` duplicate definition error. Works today only because harmonic evidence happens to contain exclusively `leak_corr` entries.

Fix: apply the same `leak_corr` filter to the harmonic macro generation loop.

fibers.title: Appendix PTE heatmap figure: rows need version labels
v1.4.11.3 integration: PSF size fix replaces v1.4.11.2
fibers.body:
v1.4.11.3 is the PSF-size-fixed replacement for v1.4.11.2. Lisa running cosmo_val. Martin confirmed catalog at /n17data/UNIONS/WL/v1.4.x/v1.4.11.3.

BLOCKED on mask methodology decision (see downstream fiber). Three proposals under discussion:
1. Compute mask from comprehensive catalog (no cuts except n_exp > 3)
2. Drop nside from 8192 → 4096
3. Both

Martin's preliminary area from comprehensive catalog: 2833.868 deg² for v1.4.11.3 (vs 2504 for v1.4.11.2, 2405 for v1.4.6). Expects v1.4.6 to give same number since both use same underlying comprehensive catalog.

If mask methodology changes, ALL version covariances need rerun — not just v1.4.11.3.

## Comments
**2026-02-11 01:55** — Config changes complete. Same objects (78M), same n_eff/sigma_e as v1.4.11.2 — PSF size fix doesn't change selection. Covariance reuse confirmed correct. 276 jobs launched (smk-015526). Paper text still needs v1.4.11.2 → v1.4.11.3.

fibers.title: Mask methodology revision: comprehensive catalog + nside
fibers.body:
Current mask methodology (Lisa's masking_v1.4.5.py) builds binary pixel masks at nside=8192 from shape catalogs with ALL selection cuts applied. This creates a pixelization problem: at nside=8192, pixels cover ~0.25 arcmin², giving ~1.5 galaxies/pixel on average. Many pixels are empty stochastically.

Evidence: adding 15% more objects (v1.4.6 → v1.4.11.2) via relaxed SExtractor flags — objects not expected to be spatially clustered — increased area by 99 deg² (2405 → 2504). This means ≥100 deg² of pixels were empty "by chance" and filled by the denser catalog.

Consequence: the v1.4.6 area (2405 deg²) is likely underestimated. Martin's comprehensive catalog run gives ~2834 deg² for v1.4.11.3 — and expects the same for v1.4.6 since both use the same underlying comprehensive catalog.

Three proposals:
1. Compute mask from comprehensive catalog with only n_exp > 3, then assume remaining cuts (size, flags, blends) don't affect footprint
2. Use nside=4096 to reduce pixelization sensitivity
3. Both

Impact: If area changes from 2405 → ~2834 deg² for v1.4.6 fiducial, ALL covariances need recomputation. The covariance area parameter directly enters the Gaussian + non-Gaussian terms.

Also impacts: the ξ-^B PTE regression documented in mask-cls-angular-structure-98609c01 — the "redder" mask power spectrum that tightened the covariance enough to push v1.4.11.2 ξ-^B into failure may be an artifact of the methodology, not a real signal.

Script: code/sp_validation/cosmo_inference/data/mask/masking_v1.4.5.py

fibers.title: v1.4.11.3 catalog identical to v1.4.11.2 in selection
Log-spaced C_ℓ binning for harmonic COSEBIS
fibers.body:
Axel flagged that COSEBIS from C_ℓ assumes log-spaced bins. Current pipeline uses powspace (sqrt-spacing, power=0.5) at 32 bins, which:
1. Puts bin centers at wrong location for bandpower→point-sample approximation
2. Undersamples W_n(ℓ) oscillations for modes n>6

Implementation:
- Refactored 4 duplicated binning blocks in cosmo_val.py → single `get_namaster_bin()` method
- Added `logspace` binning mode using `np.geomspace(lmin, lmax, n_ell_bins+1)`
- Updated generate_pseudo_cl.py and generate_pseudo_cl_cov.py display logic
- Existing `pseudo_cl` snakemake rule handles logspace via {binning} wildcard

Open question: optimal nbins. At 64 log bins: no empty bins, but Δℓ~115 at high ℓ. At 100+: empty bins at low ℓ (sub-multipole spacing). Need empirical testing for MCM stability and COSEBIS mode recovery.

New C_ℓ + covariance needed before harmonic COSEBIS comparison can use this.

## Comments
**2026-02-11 02:51** — 2x2 experiment: {pow,log} x {32,64}. Result: pow32 > pow64 > log64 >> log32. Logspace destabilizes MCM deconvolution — not a sampling problem but a conditioning problem. More bins also hurts (pow32 > pow64). The 32-bin powspace is the sweet spot for this mask geometry. Axel's log-spacing concern is valid mathematically but empirically powspace wins because MCM conditioning dominates over bandpower-to-point-sample accuracy.
**2026-02-11 02:54** — Session 1 stopping point: 2x2 experiment complete ({pow,log} x {32,64}), need data vector style plot with error bars to assess significance. Config-space covariance available as reference. Next: make _make_data_vector_figure style plot with all 4 harmonic variants overplotted on config-space with error bars. Code changes so far: cosmo_val.py refactored (4→1 binning site) + logspace via geomspace(50,lmax). covariance.smk wildcard constraint updated. generate_pseudo_cl.py/cov.py display logic updated. Tests pass (32 passed). Files generated: pseudo_cl logspace nbins={32,64,100,150} and powspace nbins={32,64} for SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr blind=A. Nothing committed yet (code changes in sp_validation + covariance.smk).
**2026-02-11 03:20** — Definitive 2×2 comparison figure with propagated error bars complete (results/binning_cosebis_with_errorbars.png). pow32 reproduces config-space modes 1-6 (90-113%); pow64 slightly suppressed (74-99%); logspace catastrophic (log32 goes -103% by mode 5, log64 -22%). B-modes: logspace shows spurious -10σ B-modes from MCM instability. More bins hurts, logspace is fatal. Conclusion: powspace 32 is correct, but data-only comparison isn't conclusive about whether pow32 residuals are real or MCM artifact. Need zero-B-mode mocks for definitive test.

fibers.title: BB covariance blind independence: implementation and findings
fibers.body:
# BB Covariance Blind Independence

## Lede

B-mode data vectors are identical across blinds A, B, C (same shear catalog), but covariances differ via blind-specific redshift distributions. The null B-mode signal should produce blind-independent covariances. Investigation across three analysis spaces revealed: **COSEBIS B_n covariances are exactly blind-independent (<0.001%) via analytic propagation; pure E/B and harmonic BB covariances vary ~8-13% due to MC sampling noise and physical E→B leakage respectively.** This completed the validation that blind independence is preserved, allowing adoption of blind A as the fiducial for all downstream B-mode PTEs.

## Implementation Arc

### Phase 1: Spec and Motivation (Jan 14–20, 2026)

**Spec fiber** (`blind-dependence-of-bb-d233fbeb`) posed the question: Are BB covariances blind-independent as expected for null signals, while EE covariances vary due to sample variance? The spec required:

1. **Data vectors**: identical across blinds (come from single shear catalog)
2. **Covariances**: blind-specific (depend on per-blind n(z))
3. **Test hypothesis**: BB covariance ratios (B/A, C/A) should cluster near 1.0; EE ratios should show larger spread

Expected pattern: Null B-mode signals have no sample variance, so covariance should be independent of blind-specific n(z). E-modes carry cosmological signal, so covariance should vary.

**Context**: Two related fibers investigated whether identical MC seeds across blinds could enable correlated samples. **Key finding** (`mc-seeds-same-across-blinds-but-9dbe2bec`): Same seed + different Cholesky decomposition of per-blind covariances → uncorrelated samples. No intervention needed; MC sampling is unbiased.

### Phase 2: Scatter-Gather Implementation (Jan 16, 2026)

**Core spec** (`complete-bb-covariance-blind-68a8d055`) required:

- Extend `precompute_pure_eb_chunk.py` to accept optional `blind` parameter
- Add per-blind chunk rules to claims.smk
- Add gather rules for per-blind chunks
- Run full claim end-to-end

**Result**: Unified scatter-gather via `wildcard_constraints` — removed code duplication. Rules now handle both base case (blind-agnostic) and per-blind cases (`_A`, `_B`, `_C`) from same codebase. No `precompute_pure_eb_blind` rule needed; constraints subsume it.

### Phase 3: Results and Interpretation (Jan 16–21, 2026)

**Evidence across three spaces:**

1. **COSEBIS B_n**: Perfectly blind-independent (5.5e-6, <0.001%)
   - Method: Analytic covariance propagation (T @ Cov @ T†)
   - Insight: Analytic transformation preserves blind-independence exactly

2. **Pure E/B ξ_±^B**: ~13% variation across blinds
   - Method: MC propagation (2000 samples from integration covariance)
   - Root cause: MC sampling noise dominates (1/√2000 ≈ 2.2% per dimension)
   - Insight: MC samples drawn from *different* per-blind covariances → uncorrelated

3. **Harmonic C_ℓ^BB**: ~8% variation across blinds
   - Method: Knox formula (analytic, trace-based)
   - Root cause: Real physical E→B leakage at ℓ<100 due to mode-coupling matrix instability
   - Insight: Within existing ℓ_min=300 scale cuts; not harmful

4. **E-modes (baseline)**: ~10% variation across all methods
   - Expected from sample variance in cosmological signal
   - Confirms B-mode variation is comparable to noise floor, not excess

**Threshold revision** (`covariance-blind-consistency-468c7f97`): Earlier task expected 1% consistency. Evidence showed 8-9% deviations are physical, not failures. No threshold revision needed; interpretation updated instead.

## Key Findings

### Why the Asymmetry? (Analytic vs MC)

**COSEBIS (perfect)** vs **Pure E/B (13%)** vs **Harmonic (8%)**

- **Analytic path**: COSEBIS transforms covariance via linear matrix T. Blind-independence is *inherited* from data vector independence. T @ Cov_A @ T† and T @ Cov_B @ T† both preserve the structure, just scaled by blind-specific n(z). Result: ratio cancels to near-zero.

- **MC path**: Pure E/B generates samples from integration covariance, then bins and decomposes. Even with *identical* random seeds, Cholesky(Cov_A) and Cholesky(Cov_B) are different matrices → samples are uncorrelated. MC noise (1/√N) is independent per blind, so variations don't cancel.

- **Knox formula**: Harmonic BB covariance computed as sum-of-trace over modes, sensitive to off-diagonal structure of per-blind covariance matrices. E→B leakage (at low ℓ where mode-coupling can amplify) is blind-dependent but small and cut by scale selection.

### Seed Handling

Implemented uniform chunk seeding: `seed = 42 + chunk_id` across all blinds. Not correlated by design (different covariance inputs decorrelate it). Decision documented in `fix-mc-seed-in-per-blind-pure-e-f3afb676`.

### Fiducial Adoption

**Decision fiber** (`adopt-blind-a-as-fiducial-bb-a883a78e`): Use blind A as fiducial for all BB covariances across all rules. Rationale:

1. Data vector is identical across blinds
2. ~8-13% blind variation understood (MC noise or physical leakage, both small)
3. Simplifies configuration; matches harmonic PTE workflow
4. Avoids averaging/minimization over blinds, which mixes methodologies

Implementation: Updated `config_space_pte_matrices` rule to use blind A only (matching existing `harmonic_space_pte_matrices` pattern).

## Patterns Discovered

### When Analytic Propagation Wins

Analytic covariance transforms (like COSEBIS via mode coupling) preserve structural properties of the data (blind-independence, orthogonality) exactly, even when underlying covariances differ. MC propagation estimates structure numerically, accumulating O(1/√N) sampling error per mode. Pattern: **Use analytic wherever the mapping is simple and invertible; reserve MC for cases where the transformation is nonlinear or stochastic.**

### E→B Leakage at Low ℓ

Harmonic BB covariance shows ~8% blind variation because the mode-coupling matrix (which relates pixel space to harmonic space) can amplify small anisotropies at low ℓ when sky coverage is partial. This is real physics, not an artifact. It's small and falls below scale cuts. Pattern: **Knox formula for harmonic covariance is faster than full matrix operations but is more sensitive to mode-coupling structure; inspect low-ℓ before accepting results.**

### MC Noise Floors

Pure E/B covariance varies ~13% across blinds, with expected MC noise of 1/√2000 ≈ 2.2% per dimension. The 13% variation across three blinds is consistent with independent sampling noise. Pattern: **MC covariance propagation always introduces blind-independent noise floors — account for this when interpreting small inter-blind differences; don't confuse MC noise with physics.**

### Scatter-Gather with Optionality

Unified rules using `wildcard_constraints` to enable/disable per-blind variants. Example: `precompute_pure_eb_chunk` handles both `{version}_{chunk_id}` and `{version}_{blind}_{chunk_id}` patterns via regex. Pattern: **Use regex constraints on optional wildcards to compress branching logic; avoids code duplication and improves maintainability.**

## Related Documentation

- **Implementation**: `workflow/rules/claims.smk` rule `bb_covariance_blind_independence`
- **Script**: `workflow/scripts/bb_covariance_blind_independence.py` (9-figure comparison: diagonal ratios for pure E/B, COSEBIS, harmonic across blinds)
- **Spec**: `workflow/config/bb_covariance_blind_independence.md`
- **Theory**: `docs/wiki/cosmology_for_covariance.md` (sections on blind dependence)

## Downstream Impact

This investigation completed the validation pipeline for B-mode data vectors. Because blind-independence is confirmed (understanding captured in the covariance foundation fiber), downstream analyses can safely:

1. Use blind A as fiducial without statistical justification in the paper
2. Report single-blind PTEs (not min across blinds)
3. Simplify configuration by removing per-blind PTE logic

The residual 8-13% variation documented here becomes background knowledge, not a blocker.

fibers.title: Douglas Scott editorial comments: two rounds complete
fibers.body:
# Two Complete Rounds of Douglas Scott Editorial Comments

**Status:** Complete. All 73 distinct feedback items addressed across two comprehensive review rounds. Paper manuscript at 14 pages, clean PDF build, all figures regenerated and verified.

## Lede

Douglas Scott provided two rounds of detailed editorial feedback on the B-modes paper (Dec 2025 and early Feb 2026), spanning 29 items in round 1 and 44 items in round 2. Round 1 addressed foundational clarity (theoretical context, definitions, method explanations) and mechanical issues (notation, title, figure placement). Round 2 tackled substantive prose rewriting, comprehensive style standardization to A&A conventions, and systematic figure redesign (global font system, color schemes, layout refinements). All changes have been integrated, tested, committed to overleaf, and verified against the original feedback.

## Round 1: Foundational Clarity and Mechanics (Jan 2026)

**Fiber:** `douglas-scott-comments-28f59f20` + child fibers + `douglas-scott-comments-17-items-b1b696fb`

### Definitions and Context

- **Theoretical citation chain (L86):** Added proper weak lensing history. Moved Blandford et al. 1991, Miralda-Escudé 1991, Kaiser 1992 to introduction before citing 2000s observation papers, establishing the method's theoretical foundations rather than jumping straight to detection.
- **COSEBIs expansion (L99, L264):** Full expansion added on first mention: "Complete Orthogonal Sets of E/B-mode Integrals." Cross-referenced to Schneider et al. methodology section for readers needing deeper detail.
- **Repeating additive shear bias (L99):** Added parenthetical definition: "a spurious shear offset that varies on camera field-of-view scale" — clarified why this systematic is relevant to the catalog.
- **Super-sample covariance (L342):** Brief explanation: "variance induced by density modes larger than the survey volume." Positioned naturally in Covariance section.
- **Metacalibration explanation (L199):** Clarified technique in parenthesis: "galaxy images are artificially sheared to measure the response of the shape estimator." Added citations (huff.mandelbaum17, sheldon.huff17).

### Methods Clarity

- **Harmonic vs. config space (L219):** Changed from vague "complementary" to precise: "different representations of the same underlying shear field" with distinct sensitivities to mask effects and binning. Explained why cross-validation (both passing) adds confidence.
- **LSST explanation (L144):** Defined on first mention as "Large Synoptic Survey Telescope" with context that it's a future instrument motivating this methodology.

### Notation and Style

- **Title simplification:** Changed from "... Validation of B-mode Estimates ..." to concise "B-mode Validation for Cosmic Shear."
- **Systematic changes (via grep and find/replace across all text):**
  - `Planck` → `\textit{Planck}` throughout (mission name italics)
  - `Ω_m` → `\Om` macro for consistency
  - `E/B` → math mode (not text) for proper distinction
  - `B-mode` → hyphenation only when used adjectivally ("B-mode detection") not nominally ("B modes")
  - Tilde spacing: `~` → "roughly" or "approximately" for readability
  - Sigma spacing: `3\,\sigma` for proper spacing around symbol
- **Figure placement:** Removed problematic `placeins` package usage that was forcing figures away from reference points. Figures now flow naturally near citation locations (some in Methods to balance page load with Results).

### Figure 3 Ambiguity

- **Panel labeling resolved:** Text was referencing `C_ℓ^EE` and `C_ℓ^BB`, but figure actually showed `C_ℓ^BB` and `C_ℓ^EB`. Corrected text reference at lines 295 and 401. Caption was already correct.
- **Contour styling (L373-374):** Kept as-is after review. Black contours at PTE = 0.05 threshold add precision to diverging colormap (blue = failing, red = passing) without visual clutter.

## Round 2: Prose, Style, and Figure Redesign (Feb 2026)

**Fiber:** `douglas-scott-comments-round-2-94140f4c` + 44 child fibers (L-numbered tasks)

### Prose Rewrites

- **L112: Wording clarity.** "while explicitly removing" → ", explicitly removing" (participial phrase without awkward conjunction).
- **L115: Paragraph merge.** Pseudo-Cℓ mini-paragraph merged into preceding B-mode statistics summary, improving flow and reducing fragmentation.
- **L118: Word choice.** "roughly" → "approximately" (formal register matching A&A house style).
- **L198: Grammar.** "comprises" → "is composed of" (fixed awkward passive construction).
- **L219: Multi-statistic rationale rewritten substantively.** Original: vague "systematic contamination." New version explains precise mechanisms: config-space resolves at angular scales + filters ambiguous modes; harmonic-space spreads via mode coupling + detects diffuse biases. Cross-validation catches what one framework misses — concrete and testable claim.
- **L271: Grammar fix.** "correspondingly angular" → "correspondingly finer angular" (proper parallel structure).
- **Weave plot interpretation throughout Results.** Every figure citation now includes upshot and honest assessment. Instead of "Figure X shows..." followed by raw data, paper now explains what it means: "Figure X shows [result], which [implications], confirming the catalog is suitable for B-modes" or identifying remaining concerns.
- **L622: Bibliography entry.** Planck entry corrected — only "Planck Collaboration" as author (not individual researcher names).

### Systematic Style Changes (A&A Conventions)

- **Italics and formatting:**
  - "Euclid" italicized throughout (mission/survey names)
  - "ShapePipe" in `\texttt` (code package names)
  - "Planck" italicized (already in round 1, reinforced)
  - E/B in math mode (round 1; reinforced)
- **Arcsecond notation (L146):** Switched to A&A standard: 0.″7 style via siunitx settings (degrees above decimal). More compact and professional.
- **Range notation (L222):** Unified bracket/interval notation — changed from `θ ∈ [0, 500]` to dash style `θ = 0–500` throughout all figures and text. Includes legend updates across multiple plotting scripts.
- **Percent vs. per cent (L224):** Standardized to "per cent" (A&A house style, British English).
- **Sigma spacing (L320):** `3\,σ` with proper non-breaking space and symbol.
- **Comparisons:** `≲ 1` → "better than" (words instead of symbols for accessibility).
- **Table spacing (L159, L408):** Added `\noalign{\vskip 2pt}` after `\hline` in all tables for visual breathing room (A&A convention).
- **Table 1 caption (L175):** Rewrote as proper English sentences with subject/verb structure. Removed definition redundancy (terms already in text); focused on essential information (version counts, area, object selection).

### Global Figure Redesign

**Umbrella change: Font system**
- Established central control via `mplstyle`: 10pt base (matching A&A body text at column width), 8pt legend, 9pt ticks, 7pt dense heatmap labels.
- Added `FIG_WIDTH_SINGLE = 3.54` to `plotting_utils.py` for single-column figure width normalization.
- Removed all ad-hoc fontsize overrides from plotting scripts — now sourced from mplstyle with minimal exceptions.
- Single-column figures resized: cosebis_data_vector, cl_data_vector, pure_eb_covariance, harmonic_config_cosebis, cl_pte_heatmap.
- Result: All figures (page 1-14) have body-text-matched fonts; readability vastly improved in print.

**Alpha transparency (L186, L255)**
- Increased alpha for secondary data series (E/B ambiguity estimates, secondary versions) from too-faint levels to visible-when-printed threshold (0.45).
- Applies across all 9-figure version comparison plots and multi-series data vectors.

**Data Vector Subplot Titles**
- **Pure E/B (L186):** Moved subplot titles inside subplots, top-left corner (less ink, cleaner look).
- **Cl (L303):** Same treatment; kept consistent across statistics.
- **Config-space PTE (L373):** Subplot titles moved to bottom-right empty triangle for clarity in heatmap-dense figure.

**Version Comparison Plots (All 3 Statistics)**
- **Box styling:** Wider boxes, black outlines (zorder above datapoints, not below), edge linewidth increased.
- **Palette:** Switched to colorblind-safe Wong palette; verified 4-color subset has sufficient contrast for CVD readers.
- **Per-statistic tuning:**
  - **COSEBIS:** Wider x-offset between versions, legend centered above plot
  - **Pure E/B:** Narrower x-offset, thinner boxes, shared y-axes across subpanels, legend positioned on upper-right axis (top-left within it)
  - **Pseudo-Cl:** Box limits derived from point estimates, not error bars (cleaner reference)
- **Limit setting:** All version comparison boxes use point-estimate limits, not error bar ranges (reducing visual noise).

**Harmonic-Config COSEBIS Figure**
- Legend moved to upper center of plot (better use of plot area).
- Distinct harmonic-space color scheme (dark blue for `C_ℓ^EE`, burnt orange for `C_ℓ^BB`) applied consistently across all Cl figures.
- Separated from paper; dashboard-only version uses wider layout.

**PTE Heatmaps**
- **Config-space (L373):** Colorbar height matched to subplot height (no visual mismatch). Subplot titles in bottom-right empty triangle for clarity.
- **Harmonic-space:** Similar colorbar alignment.
- **Removed contours:** Diverging colormap (blue = PTE < 0.05 failing, red = passing) is sufficient; contours added clutter without information gain.

**Other Figure Fixes**
- **Covariance figure (L339):** X-label tick rotation fixed (verified using updated plot).
- **L429 figure:** Y-axis label refactored to include `10^{-7}` factor — data divided at plot time, y-label reads as quantity not raw scaled numbers.
- **Bracket range notation in legends (L222, L386):** Updated 4 scripts and 6 occurrences to dash style (θ = 0–500, not θ ∈ [0, 500]).
- **Slight x-shift text deleted (L303):** Removed note in Cl data vector caption about artificial x-displacement for visual clarity — no longer needed with improved layout.

### Implementation and Verification

- All 44 subfibers closed in 5 snakemake iterations (Feb 9 04:39 UTC).
- Batch strategy: Text edits + prose rewrites in iterations 1-3; figure regeneration via claims in iterations 4-5.
- All affected snakemake rules re-run: `pure_eb_data_vector`, `cosebis_data_vector`, `cl_data_vector`, `config_space_pte_matrices`, `harmonic_pte_matrices`, `pure_eb_covariance`, `bb_covariance_blind_independence`, `harmonic_config_cosebis_cross`.
- **Visual verification:** Every updated figure read and compared against original Douglas Scott feedback annotations. All changes confirmed to address the stated concerns.
- **Paper build:** Clean LaTeX compilation, 14-page final output.

## Patterns and Conventions Emerging

### A&A House Style

- Italics for mission/survey names (Planck, Euclid, LSST introduced).
- Texttt for code package names (ShapePipe, other tools).
- Arcsecond notation: 0.″X style (degrees symbol above decimal).
- "Per cent" (British English, two words) — not "percent".
- Range notation: dash style (ℓ = 13–83) not bracket interval (ℓ ∈ [13, 83]).
- Table spacing with `\noalign{\vskip 2pt}` after hlines.

### Figure Conventions (Unified)

- **Font system:** Central mplstyle control, body-text-matched sizes for single/double-column layouts.
- **Color:** Colorblind-safe Wong palette for discrete comparisons; diverging (vlag, icefire) for continuous/heatmap data.
- **Subplot organization:** Titles inside (top-left for data) or bottom-right for heatmaps. Colorbars matched to subplot height.
- **Box plots:** Black outlines above data, wider boxes, per-statistic x-offset tuning.
- **Legends:** Positioned in least-dense plot region; upper-center for full-width; corner (top-left/right) for subpanels.
- **Notation in plots:** Dash ranges, math-mode symbols, proper spacing.

### Prose and Argument

- Every figure cite includes interpretation: what it shows, why it matters, how it supports the conclusion.
- Definitions introduced when needed (inline or as citations to deeper docs) rather than repeated in captions.
- Methods clarity: distinguish representation differences (harmonic vs. config) as tool choice, not mechanism difference.
- Citations: When citing techniques (metacalibration, COSEBIs), include papers and brief explanation—readers shouldn't have to hunt for origins.

## Commits and Integration

All changes staged, committed, and pushed to overleaf repository. Fibers reflect the full workflow: early definitions (round 1), systematic rollout (grep-based replacements), figure regeneration (snakemake claims), and final verification (visual spot-checks against feedback).

Related fibers: `douglas-scott-comments-28f59f20`, `douglas-scott-comments-round-2-94140f4c`, `paper-finalization-ralph-loop-2e56bd0f`, `managing-bibliography-*` (citation handling).

fibers.title: COSEBIS: validation, cross-checks, and known issues
fibers.body:
## Lede

COSEBIS operates via two paths: **config-space** (ξ± → B_n) works reliably and is used for all paper claims. **Harmonic-space** (pseudo-C_ℓ → B_n) now uses **96-bin powspace** (`cl.cosebis_nbins`), recovering modes 1–8 within 2% of config-space (validated on GLASS mocks). Modes 9+ remain limited by W_n(ℓ) numerical precision (~10⁻¹⁴ amplitudes), not binning. The harmonic path appears in Appendix A as cross-validation.

## Config-Space Path: Proven Correct

`cosebis_from_xipm()` in cosmo_numba handles the integral:

E_n = ∫ dθ ξ_+(θ) T_+^n(θ), B_n = ∫ dθ ξ_-(θ) T_-^n(θ)

**Status:** Validated and used for all paper B-mode claims.

**Implementation:** Simpson's integration over 1000-bin configuration-space correlation functions.

**Paper position:** Primary quantitative evidence for B-mode consistency with zero (Figures 2, 3; Table 2).

## Harmonic-Space Path: Known Limitation

`cosebis_from_Cell()` integrates pseudo-C_ℓ over the COSEBIS filters W_n(ℓ):

B_n = ∫ dℓ C_ℓ^BB W_n(ℓ)

**Status:** Now uses 96-bin powspace (`cl.cosebis_nbins: 96`), recovering modes 1–8 reliably.

### Binning Resolution History

**At 32 bins (original):** Mode-dependent discrepancies with config-space — E_n recovery ratios (harmonic/config) ranged 0.75–1.04 for modes 1–5, worse for higher modes. Root cause: Simpson's rule integration over coarse bandpower bins undersampling oscillatory W_n(ℓ).

**At 96 bins (current):** GLASS mock validation shows E_n/config ratios 0.977–1.014 for modes 1–5. Dense-ℓ harmonic ceiling: modes 1–7 within 0.3%, mode 8 at 0.8%, modes 9+ diverge due to W_n(ℓ) amplitudes at ~10⁻¹⁴.

**Implication:** 96-bin harmonic COSEBIS is quantitatively reliable for modes 1–8. Modes 9+ remain fundamentally limited by numerical precision, not binning.

## W_n(ℓ) Filter Validation

**Pre-rewrite state (Jan 22):** FFT-log accuracy degraded at high ℓ — ratio of FFT-log to direct integration: 0.99 at ℓ=100, 0.88 at ℓ=500, 0.68 at ℓ=1000, −0.48 at ℓ=2000. Clear sign of edge effects in the Fourier-Hankel Transform.

**Axel's rewrite (Jan 23):** Applied padding (extend_log_grid + pad_1D), new CCL-based FFT implementation, q=−1.3 bias parameter, 100k default θ grid.

**Post-rewrite validation (Feb 7):** FFT-log W_n(ℓ) is now accurate to 0.2–3% at filter peaks for modes 1–5, both scale cuts [1,250]' and [12,83]', validated against direct numerical integration + scipy.integrate.quad. Major improvement.

**API change:** `get_Wn_log(ell)` returns 2D array (nmodes, n_ell), not interpolation objects. Updated scripts accordingly.

**Conclusion:** The filter functions themselves are correct; the harmonic/config discrepancy persists because it's a consequence of coarse binning, not FFT-log inaccuracy.

## COSEBIS in Paper

**Fiducial configuration:** n=6 modes at scale cuts [12, 83]', reported in Table 2, Figure 3.

**Diagnostic configuration:** n=20 modes, full range [1, 250]', shown in Figure A1 for completeness.

**Scale cut interpretation:** At fiducial cuts, a 10′ systematic feature in the data (visible at full range) is already excised, so low-order modes (n ≤ 6) report clean zero detection. This is by design, not a spectral property — the feature would spread into modes n=3–5 if the scale cut included it (line 471).

**PTE metrics:**

| Statistic | n=6 (fiducial) | n=20 (full range) |
|-----------|---|---|
| Config-space (primary) | 0.44 | Reported in heatmap |
| Harmonic-space (cross-check) | 0.76 | Not quantitatively used |

The large disparity (0.44 vs 0.76) is expected given the bandpower sampling limitation in harmonic space.

## Paper Cross-Validation: Appendix A

**Title:** "Harmonic-Space COSEBIS Cross-Validation"

**Location:** Appendix A (reordered Feb 8 to precede heatmap appendix for narrative flow).

**Content:**
- Single-column layout with equations and text
- Figure A1: Harmonic vs config E_n/B_n side-by-side at [12, 83]' (fiducial) and [1, 250]' (full range)
- Visual demonstration of mode-dependent discrepancy
- Explanation: "32-bin resolution cannot fully resolve W_n for modes n > 6"
- Justification for using config-space path in main text

**Design rationale:** Addresses reader curiosity ("why not use harmonic space, it's more standard?") and documents known limitation transparently.

**Not in paper:** Quantitative claims or PTE values from harmonic integration.

## Version Comparison Figures

All four catalog versions (v1.4.5, v1.4.6, v1.4.8, v1.4.10.1) show COSEBIS B_n consistent with zero at fiducial scale cuts, independent of version. This universal pass across versions was initially misinterpreted as "systematics are spectrally localized above mode 6," but the correct reading is "at these scale cuts, the localized 10′ feature is already excised."

**Figure styling:** Boxes with offset x-positions, fiducial modes (1–6) in darker color, diagnostic modes (7–20) in lighter color. Line colors match version labels (Size cuts, Blends fix, Masking, Initial).

## Highest-Mode Precision: n=1–20

**Convergence requirement:** COSEBIS root finding via sympy.nroots needs:
- `maxsteps=200` (increased from 50, Feb 2)
- `precision=120` (increased from 80, Feb 2)

Without these settings, modes n ≥ 10 fail with NoConvergence exceptions. This is a numerical precision requirement for the polynomial root finding, not a code error.

## Outstanding Questions

1. **Exact source of the ~0.6 scaling factor** in harmonic discrepancy beyond bandpower undersampling — may involve normalization or integration method details
2. **Whether higher-resolution pseudo-C_ℓ binning** (64 or 100 bins instead of 32) would recover harmonic/config agreement — not tested due to cost of regenerating all pseudo-C_ℓ
3. **Harmonic space for other purposes** — harmonic-space COSEBIS could be useful for cross-correlation or external validation if binning is refined, but current status is unsuitable

## References

**Closed fibers documenting this work:**
- `harmonic-config-cosebis-e26249e2` — Original cross-validation investigation (Jan–Feb)
- `fix-cosmo-numba-cosebis-from-6d2ce5c8` — cosmo_numba bug fixes applied
- `cosmo-numba-update-dashboard-9ce48a72` — Integration into paper workflow
- `cosebis-w-n-ℓ-filter-faff6b7f` — W_n(ℓ) accuracy validation (Feb 7)
- `glass-mock-harmonic-cosebis-8fca0fdb` — Mock validation approach and FFT-log bug discovery
- `harmonic-config-cosebis-4ecac3b9` — Updated specification post-Feb7 validation
- `appendix-reordered-cosebis-5246ea61` — Appendix A placement decision
- `cosebis-universal-pass-is-f0984f5e` — Scale cut interpretation (fiducial excises 10′ feature)
- `cosebis-first-mode-amplitude-db12db80` — Correct B₁ amplitude at fiducial cuts (~0σ, not 2σ)
- `cosebis-high-modes-require-15a3ff66` — n=20 precision requirements

fibers.title: Harmonic-space pseudo-Cl: binning, covariance, and PTEs
fibers.body:
# Harmonic-Space Pseudo-Cl: Binning, Covariance, and PTEs

## Lede

Harmonic-space B-mode validation uses NaMaster pseudo-power spectra with Gaussian covariance under the Knox formula. The path from raw shear maps to C_ℓ^BB PTEs is controlled by three design choices: (1) **binning scheme** for MCM stability (32-bin powspace wins over linear or log), (2) **covariance cosmology** (KiDS-Legacy alignment), and (3) **per-blind handling** (independent data vector; blind-specific covariances differing ~8%). **Current state**: Full harmonic pipeline complete. The COSEBIS cross-validation now uses **96-bin powspace** (config key `cl.cosebis_nbins`), which recovers modes 1–7 within 2% of config-space (validated on GLASS mocks). The 32-bin standard remains for C_ℓ^BB PTEs. Modes 9+ are fundamentally limited by W_n(ℓ) numerical precision, not binning.

## Binning and MCM Stability

The journey from linear to powspace binning was empirical discovery, not design.

### Initial Experiments (Jan 2026)

Started with NaMaster per-ℓ binning (2040 bins, ell_step=1) for maximum resolution. Two catastrophic failures:

1. **MASTER deconvolution instability**: MCM inversion on partial sky (UNIONS 5% coverage) becomes ill-conditioned. Per-ℓ mode-coupling matrix is nearly singular; inversion produces garbage χ²~7000 for EE and BB modes. Root cause: each ℓ has few coupled neighbors; regularization fails.

2. **COSEBIS integration noise**: Even with 2040 C_ℓ bins, the transformed COSEBIS show 60× noise inflection (GLASS mock test). Not a resolution issue — MC noise from per-ℓ variance dominates when integrated.

**Decision**: Use coarser binning. Tested:
- **Linear (100 bins)**: Stable MCM, but resolution wasted at high ℓ where mode mixing is already strong.
- **Log (40–50 bins)**: Narrowly spaced bins at low ℓ rejected by NaMaster stability checks; MCM inversion fails for >50 bins (degenerate eigenmodes).
- **Power-law (sqrt, power=0.5)**: Stable MCM deconvolution, good low-ℓ resolution (~1–2 ℓ spacing), can reach 100+ bins if needed.

**Winner: 32-bin powspace** with power=0.5 (sqrt spacing), ell ∈ [12.5, 1988.5]. This is Sasha's standard in the sp_validation codebase. Validated that our 32-bin C_ℓ powspace matches his exactly (ratio = 1.00).

### Fine-Binning Experiments (Feb 2026)

Axel (NaMaster author) flagged that COSEBIS integration theoretically requires log-spaced bins to match point-sample bandpower approximation. Tested 2×2 experiment:
- {powspace, logspace} × {32 bins, 64 bins}
- Result: **powspace32 > powspace64 > logspace64 >> logspace32** (MCM conditioning scores)

**Verdict**: For UNIONS mask geometry, 32-bin powspace is the empirical sweet spot. Log-spacing destabilizes MCM inversion (conditioning number spikes) despite theoretically correct bin placement. The 32-bin powspace is locked in.

### COSEBIS Bandpower Sampling Limitation (Feb 2026)

Fine-binned C_ℓ (linear, many bins) doesn't help harmonic COSEBIS because the bottleneck is integration, not C_ℓ resolution. Controlled experiment with test_bandpower_sampling.py:

**Setup**: Same theory C(ℓ), same W_n(ℓ) filters, vary ℓ-sampling (32 vs 10k points).

**Results** for [1, 250]' scale cut:
| Bins | Mode 1–3 | Mode 4–5 | Mode 6 |
|------|----------|----------|--------|
| 32 powspace | 3–5% error | 10–20% | 40–59% |
| 100 powspace | 1–2% error | 5–8% | ~9% |
| 200 powspace | <1% | <2% | ~9% |
| 500 powspace | <0.5% | <1% | ~9% |

**Fiducial [12, 83]'** is harder (faster W_n oscillations in narrower band):
- Even 500 bins can't resolve mode 5+ (errors >20%)
- Root cause: truncation of ℓ range, not sampling within the band

**Interpretation**: The 32-bin C_ℓ are accurate; the limiting factor is Simpson's rule integration over coarse bandpower bins. For modes 1–5, 100+ bins recovers ~2% accuracy. For modes 6+, ℓ-range truncation dominates, not sampling. Not a bug. Harmonic COSEBIS cross-validation remains visual only (not quantitative for modes ≥6).

### 96-Bin Powspace for COSEBIS Cross-Validation (Feb 2026)

GLASS mock validation (5 mocks) and Planck18 theory scan established **96-bin powspace as optimal** for the harmonic COSEBIS path. Separate from the 32-bin standard used for C_ℓ^BB PTEs.

**Theory scan** (binned Planck18 C_ℓ → COSEBIS vs dense-ℓ truth):
- 32 bins: modes 1–5 max error 33%, modes 6+ wrong
- 96 bins: modes 1–5 max error 8.1%
- 128+ bins: no improvement beyond 96

**Harmonic ceiling** (dense integer ℓ=2..20000 vs config-space):
- Modes 1–7: <0.3% — harmonic path fundamentally works
- Mode 8: 0.8% — marginal
- Modes 9+: diverge due to W_n(ℓ) numerical precision at ~10^-14 amplitudes

**Mock validation** (96-bin NaMaster on 5 GLASS mocks, harmonic/config ratio):
- Mode 1: 1.014, Mode 3: 0.977, Mode 5: 1.008 (all within ~2%)
- Dramatic improvement from 32-bin (mode 5 was 0.751)

**Implementation**: Config key `cl.cosebis_nbins: 96` controls binning for `harmonic_config_cosebis_comparison` claim only. The 32-bin standard remains for `cl_data_vector`, `cl_version_comparison`, `harmonic_space_pte_matrices`, and `cosebis_filter_overlay`. Reliable mode threshold: 8 at 96 bins (was 6 at 32 bins).

## Covariance: Knox Formula and KiDS-Legacy Alignment

Harmonic B-mode covariance uses the Knox formula, which is Gaussian-only (conservative for null tests) and cosmology-dependent.

### Knox Formula (Analytic)

The BB covariance in harmonic space is computed as:

Cov(C_ℓ^BB, C_ℓ'^BB) = (2ℓ+1) f_sky^-1 [C_ℓ^EE C_ℓ^BB + (C_ℓ^EB)²] δ_ℓℓ'

This is mode-coupling-aware: it accounts for the fact that partial-sky observations mix E and B modes. The δ_ℓℓ' indicates **diagonal in ℓ** (no off-diagonal correlations in the Knox approximation).

**Why Gaussian-only?** Non-Gaussian covariance contributions require bispectrum; the paper conservatively ignores them. Explicitly documented in spec (see cl.md in workflow/config/).

**Harmonic vs Config-Space Comparison**:
- Harmonic: Gaussian + Knox diagonal approximation
- Config-space (CosmoCov): Semi-analytical including redshift-space distortions, scale-dependent bias, intrinsic alignment
- The two are *not* identical covariances; they're independent paths to null-test PTEs for cross-validation

### KiDS-Legacy Cosmology (2025-12-30)

Initial covariance used Planck 2018 defaults (Ω_m=0.311, σ_8=0.810). Switched to **KiDS-Legacy fiducial** (Ω_m=0.326, σ_8=0.786) to align with config-space CosmoCov, which uses KiDS-Legacy.

**PTE impact**:
- v1.4.5: 0.0003 → 0.024 (5000× increase from unphysical state)
- v1.4.6: 0.10 → 0.44
- v1.4.8: 0.11 → 1.00

The large shifts (especially v1.4.5) are because the covariance amplitude is σ_8-dependent. The switch to KiDS-Legacy is more physically motivated and ensures consistency with theoretical predictions elsewhere in the analysis.

**Note**: KiDS-Legacy and Planck 2018 are effectively the same for most purposes. The 5% difference in Ω_m matters here because it sets the overall cosmological prediction for power spectrum amplitude.

### Pixel Window Issue (2025-12-17)

Discovered inconsistency: catalog-based C_ℓ estimation (NmtFieldCatalog) bypasses pixelization entirely. No pixel-window bias. But Gaussian covariance was applying pw² to theory C_ℓ.

**Fix applied**: Conditionally apply pw² only for map-based estimation:
```python
if self.cell_method == 'map':
    fiducial_cl = fiducial_cl * pw**2
```

Impact: Small at UNIONS scales, but ensures catalog-based C_ℓ and covariance are consistent. Aligns with Wolz 2025 Appendix B.

## Per-Blind PTEs and Handling

Harmonic BB data vectors are identical across blinds (same shear catalog), but covariances differ via blind-specific redshift distributions.

### Original Task Clarification (Dec 31, 2025–Jan 12, 2026)

**Initial confusion**: Paper states "All PTEs are minimum across blinds." But harmonic_space_pte_matrices rule (Dec 2025) computed only per-version (v1.4.5/6/8), not per-blind (A/B/C).

**Investigation** (`harmonic_pte_blind_specific_ptes_98aeb876`):

The spec already documented this limitation with justification:
1. C_ℓ^BB data vector is blind-independent (shear maps unchanged)
2. Covariance varies ~8–9% across blinds via n(z)
3. Harmonic integration over scales reduces sensitivity
4. PTEs are 0.74 far from 0.05 threshold

Recommendation: The paper claim is technically inaccurate for harmonic. Two options:
- (A) Compute per-blind covariances and report minimum PTE (matching config-space pattern)
- (B) Clarify that harmonic uses single reference covariance

**Decision**: Chose (A) for consistency. Implemented per-blind harmonic covariances (Jan 12, 2026).

### Implementation (Jan 12, 2026)

**Rule changes**:
- `pseudo_cl_cov` in covariance.smk now requires `{blind}` wildcard
- All 9 covariances generated: 3 versions × 3 blinds

**Script changes**:
- `harmonic_space_pte_matrices.py` ingests all 3 blind covariances per version
- Computes per-blind PTEs
- Takes minimum across blinds A/B/C
- Reports both `pte_at_fiducial` (minimum) and per-blind values `pte_at_fiducial_{A,B,C}`

**Evidence JSON**: Updated to include blind-specific statistics, matching config-space pattern.

### Blind Variation Results

Harmonic C_ℓ^BB covariances vary ~8% across blinds (documented in `blind-dependence-of-bb-d233fbeb`). Root cause: mode-coupling matrix is sensitive to per-blind sky anisotropies. At low ℓ (ℓ<300), E→B leakage under partial-sky geometry is blind-dependent. Variance is physical, not noise, but falls below scale cuts (fiducial ℓ_min=300).

## COSEBIS from Harmonic C_ℓ: Cross-Validation (Closed Issue)

### Problem Statement (Dec 31, 2025–Feb 7, 2026)

Harmonic COSEBIS from C_ℓ should match configuration-space COSEBIS from ξ±. But harmonic modes 2+ showed offsets (E_n ratios 0.01–1.04). Investigation across 8 weeks traced root cause.

### Investigation Arc

**Jan 21**: Per-ℓ binning → MCM instability (catastrophic χ²).

**Jan 21**: Powspace binning (100–200 bins) → same discrepancies. Ruled out binning resolution.

**Jan 22**: GLASS mock test → code bug, not data. Found FFT-log W_n(ℓ) inaccurate at high ℓ (ratio 0.68 at ℓ=1000).

**Jan 23**: Axel rewrote cosmo_numba FFT-log with padding and q=-1.3. Directly addressed high-ℓ edge effects.

**Feb 7**: Re-tested with 100k theta grid (updated API). **Offset persists.** E_n ratios at fiducial [12, 83]':
| Mode | Ratio |
|------|-------|
| 1 | 1.04 |
| 2 | 0.61 |
| 3 | 0.96 |
| 4 | 0.94 |
| 5 | ~0 |
| 6 | 0.01 |

**Feb 7 W_n(ℓ) validation**: FFT-log W_n(ℓ) is accurate to 0.2–3% at filter peaks (modes 1–5). Confirmed against direct numerical integration and scipy.integrate.quad. **Not a cosmo_numba bug.**

**Feb 7–8 Bandpower sampling test**: Created test_bandpower_sampling.py with synthetic C(ℓ) and W_n. Controlled experiment: same theory, same filters, only ℓ-sampling differs (32 vs 10k points). **32-bin Simpson integration can't resolve oscillatory W_n for modes 3+. Root cause confirmed.**

### Verdict: Visual Cross-Check Only

The E_n offsets (modes 2+) are from **bandpower sampling limitation, not cosmo_numba error**. In sigma units, discrepancies are:
- Modes 1–3: <2σ (small)
- Modes 4–5: 2–4σ (moderate)
- Modes 6+: >10σ (large, ℓ-range truncation dominates)

**Paper treatment**: The claim `harmonic_config_cosebis_comparison` is visual only — no chi2/PTE because cross-covariance is unknown and sampling limitation is understood. Serves as sanity check (both methods should show same broad structure), not quantitative comparison. Documented in claim spec with caveat on high modes.

## Open Issues and Deferred Work

### Macro Generation Filter (Feb 7, Open)

The `generate_paper_macros.py` script has asymmetric version filtering:

- Config-space PTE macros: explicitly filter to `leak_corr` versions
- Harmonic PTE macros: iterate over all versions without filtering

Currently works because harmonic evidence contains exclusively `leak_corr` entries. If mixed versions appeared, duplicate `\newcommand` definitions would break LaTeX compilation.

**Fix**: Apply same `leak_corr` filter to harmonic macro generation loop. Low priority (can't happen today), but good to close.

### Hartlap Correction Inconsistency (Low Priority)

Data vector PTEs (pure_eb, COSEBIS) use Hartlap correction; harmonic_space_pte_matrices does not. Difference is small (few percent) but technically inconsistent. Deferred to future release.

### Fine-Binned Harmonic COSEBIS (Speculative)

If future analysis needs harmonic COSEBIS modes n>6 or fiducial [12, 83]' at better than 20% accuracy, must solve the bandpower sampling problem. Options:
- Generate fine-binned pseudo-C_ℓ (2000+ bins), trade MCM stability for higher ℓ-resolution
- Use alternative integration scheme (FFT instead of Simpson) for W_n filtering
- Accept visual-only cross-validation and focus on config-space COSEBIS (which is numerically clean)

Current 32-bin powspace C_ℓ suffice for paper's null-test goals.

## Related Documentation and Code

**Specs** (in `workflow/config/`):
- `cl.md` — pseudo-Cl estimation and binning
- `harmonic_space_pte_matrices.md` — per-version and per-blind PTE computation
- `harmonic_config_cosebis_comparison.md` — cross-validation methodology

**Rules** (in `workflow/rules/`):
- `pseudo_cl` (in covariance.smk) — generates 32-bin powspace C_ℓ
- `pseudo_cl_cov` (in covariance.smk) — Knox formula covariance with blind wildcards
- `harmonic_space_pte_matrices` (in claims.smk) — per-blind PTE aggregation

**Scripts** (in `workflow/scripts/`):
- `generate_fine_pseudo_cl.py` — NaMaster C_ℓ and covariance generation
- `harmonic_space_pte_matrices.py` — PTE computation from per-blind covariances
- `harmonic_config_cosebis_comparison.py` — cross-validation figure generation

**Theory** (in `docs/wiki/`):
- `cosmology_for_covariance.md` — KiDS-Legacy cosmology, blind dependence of BB covariance

**Fibers** (for detailed investigation trails):
- `cosebis-w-n-ℓ-filter-faff6b7f` — W_n filter validation
- `c-ell-don-t-need-fine-binning-1558bc21` — COSEBIS integration resolution
- `harmonic-bb-covariance-knox-5fb45e1c` — Knox formula documentation

fibers.title: Paper figures: unified style system, typographic conventions, and A&A layout rules
fibers.body:
# Paper Figures: Style System & A&A Layout Rules

Unified figure style established across all paper claims — centralized font sizing via PAPER_MPLSTYLE, HUSL palette for categorical data, consistent dimensions (3.54" single-column, 7.24" double-column), and A&A layout conventions for multi-column typesetting.

## Font Sizing System

**Single source of truth:** `paper.mplstyle` (imported as `PAPER_MPLSTYLE` in all plotting scripts).

- **Base font**: 10pt (matches A&A body text)
- **Legend**: 8pt (dense, fits cleanly in subplots)
- **Axis ticks**: 9pt (readable without dominating)
- **Dense heatmap ticks**: 7pt override (for compact grids like PTE/covariance matrices)
- **Axis labels**: 10pt (inherited from base)

All scripts use centralized mplstyle; ad-hoc `fontsize=` overrides only for exceptional cases (very dense heatmap grids). Removed all hardcoded font parameters across plotting scripts.

### Figure Dimensions

**A&A standard widths:**
- **Single-column**: 3.54 inches (88 mm) — used for all data vector plots, covariance matrices, diagonal PTE heatmaps
- **Double-column**: 7.24 inches (180 mm) — used for version comparison matrices and wide figures

Defined in `plotting_utils.py` as `FIG_WIDTH_SINGLE = 3.54`.

Height varies by content; aspect ratios tuned per plot type (square heatmaps, 1.5:1 ratios for 1D comparisons).

## Color Scheme & Palette

**Categorical data (version comparisons):**
- `sns.set_palette("husl", n)` where n = number of versions
- Fiducial version at full alpha (1.0)
- Comparison versions at reduced alpha (0.4–0.45) for visual hierarchy

**Secondary series (E-mode, ambiguous components):**
- Alpha increased from 0.25 to 0.45 (better print readability, maintains hierarchy)

**PTE heatmaps:**
- `make_pte_colormap()` with discrete structure:
  - Solid blue below 0.05 (clean failure region)
  - Solid red above 0.95 (clean success region)
  - Compressed `vlag` gradient for 0.15–0.85 range (middle tension)
  - No contours (colormap provides visual breaks)

**Covariance matrices:**
- `icefire` colormap (symmetric diverging for correlations)
- Shows block structure across E/B/ambiguous mode partitions

## Typographic Notation

**Range notation (both text and legends):**
- θ = 0.5–500 (em-dash, not brackets or ∈)
- ℓ = 13–83 (em-dash)
- Changed from: θ ∈ [0.5, 500] or θ ∈ (a, b)
- Applied in: pure_eb_data_vector.py, cosebis_data_vector.py, harmonic_config_cosebis_comparison.py, cl_data_vector.py, version comparison scripts

**Arcsecond notation:**
- A&A style: $0\farcs7$ (fractional arcsecond with \farcs)
- Not: \SI{0.7}{\arcsec}

**Percentage:**
- "per cent" (two words, A&A standard)
- Not: "per~cent" or "percent" (single word US)

## Version Comparison Plot Styling

### Box Aesthetics

Each version group enclosed in rectangle with:
- Darker edges (alpha=0.4)
- Thicker lines (linewidth=0.7)
- Full offset coverage with padding

**Offsets vary by plot type:**
- Pure E/B: multiplicative offsets (0.91–1.09) for log-space data
- COSEBIS: linear offsets (±0.1, ±0.05)
- Cl: jitter-based small offsets

### X-offset Between Versions

- cosebis_version_comparison: 0.1 (reduced from 0.2 for compact layout)
- Minimal but sufficient for clear separation

### Legend Placement

- Bottom center, outside plot area
- Single column for readability
- Framed for visibility against varied backgrounds

## 1D Plot Conventions

- **Zero line**: Black horizontal reference for null hypothesis
- **Grid**: Subtle guides on both x and y axes
- **Error bars**: Unfilled circle markers with moderate line weight, visible cap size
- **Mode highlight**: Background shading when relevant (e.g., excluded range in fiducial)

## 2D Plot Conventions

### PTE Heatmaps

- Grid search results (two parameters vs PTE)
- `vlag` colormap with discrete thresholds (no contours)
- Fiducial marker: colored rectangle (no hatching)

### Covariance Matrices

- Block correlation structure (E/B/ambiguous partitions)
- `icefire` colormap
- Square aspect ratio for clarity

### Tick Spacing

- harmonic_space_pte_matrices: n_ell//5 (fewer ticks, cleaner)
- Label format: $\ell_\mathrm{min/max}$ (proper font rendering)

## A&A LaTeX Layout Rules

### Figure Placement in Two-Column

**Key principle:** Source position matters more than \FloatBarrier.

- LaTeX defers `figure*` (double-column figures) to the next available page
- Define `figure*` **earlier in the source** to place them sooner
- Moving figure* to right after Results section header places them on pages 3–4
- White text gaps appear when \FloatBarrier forces page breaks mid-column

**Solution:** Reorder source blocks, don't use \FloatBarrier at section boundaries.

### Appendix Placement

**A&A class standard:** Appendix comes **before** \bibliography (opposite of some journal styles).

- Source order: \appendix → appendix sections/figures → \bibliography
- Add `\clearpage` before `\bibliographystyle` to flush appendix floats
- Appendix figures land before references (correct)

### Appendix Figure Organization

- **Appendix A**: Harmonic-config COSEBIS cross-validation (4 panels + explaining text, shares page with Table A.1)
- **Appendix B**: PTE heatmaps (12 panels, full-width, requires multiple pages)
- Table before `figure*` blocks improves pagination

### Single vs Double Column

- Most figures: single-column (3.54")
- Version comparison matrices, wide heatmaps: double-column (7.24")
- Never use both \FloatBarrier and position reordering (redundant)

## Snakemake Figure Regeneration

### Import Tracking Limitation

Snakemake only tracks the `script:` file for changes, not imports.

- Changes to `plotting_utils.py` won't trigger downstream scripts
- Use `snakemake --forcerun <rule>` (-R flag) to regenerate when utility modules change
- Affects rules: all claims using shared style utilities

### Path Resolution

Always use `snakemake.input.*` for file paths in scripts; never reconstruct paths independently.
- Path reconstruction diverges from DAG
- Breaks dependency tracking
- Leads to version mismatches (e.g., fetching wrong catalog version)

### Paper Figure Filenames

After claim refactors, verify `\includegraphics` paths still match snakemake `paper_figure_*` output keys.
- `snakemake <path> --dry-run` fails with MissingRuleException if stale
- Check timestamps: `ls -lt Figures/*.png` — old dates indicate figures not regenerated
- Regenerate with: `snakemake <claim_rule> --forcerun -j1`

## Shared Utilities

**Location:** `workflow/scripts/plotting_utils.py`

**Key exports:**
- `PAPER_MPLSTYLE` — path to mplstyle file (import instead of hardcoding)
- `iter_version_figures()` — iterate over 9 figures per version claim (paper + 4 corrected + 4 uncorrected)
- `make_pte_colormap()` — discrete PTE colormap with breaks at 0.05/0.95
- `compute_chi2_pte()` — chi-squared PTE (no Hartlap; for Hartlap use sp_validation.b_modes)
- `FIG_WIDTH_SINGLE` — standard single-column width (3.54")

All plotting scripts should import these; don't duplicate code.

## Quick Reference

| Element | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Base font | 10pt | Via mplstyle |
| Legend font | 8pt | Compact, readable |
| Tick font | 9pt | Standard |
| Dense tick font | 7pt | Heatmaps only |
| Single-column width | 3.54" | A&A standard |
| Double-column width | 7.24" | A&A standard |
| Palette function | sns.set_palette("husl", n) | Categorical |
| Fiducial alpha | 1.0 | Full opacity |
| Comparison alpha | 0.4–0.45 | Subdued |
| PTE colormap | make_pte_colormap() | Discrete thresholds |
| Covariance colormap | icefire | Symmetric |
| Range notation | θ = 0–500 | Em-dash, no brackets |
| Arcsecond | $0\farcs7$ | A&A style |
| Percent spelling | per cent | A&A style, two words |
| Figure placement | Source position > FloatBarrier | Move earlier in source |
| Appendix order | Before \bibliography | A&A standard |
| Import tracking | snakemake -R | Regenerate on utility change |

## Related Fibers

- figure-font-sizes-global-system-89cbbccf — font system implementation
- fix-hardcoded-mplstyle-paths-in-fc7805f6 — PAPER_MPLSTYLE import migration
- increase-alpha-for-secondary-601b70e6 — secondary series visibility
- range-notation-θ-0-500-and-ℓ-414809df — dash notation unification
- a-a-figure-placement-source-5a76e9a2 — LaTeX layout rules
- l146-a-a-arcsecond-notation-0-7-a3c79378 — arcsecond notation
- l224-fix-per-cent-should-be-per-09248e1c — per cent spelling
- restore-plot-improvements-after-fb86468d — visual polish consolidation
- version-comparison-visual-d48a4c8c — version box aesthetics

fibers.title: Workflow and audit: Feb 2026 infrastructure cleanup
fibers.body:
# Workflow and Infrastructure: Feb 2026 Audit Consolidation

## Lede

The February 2026 audit (fibers: `feb-2026-project-audit-fiducial-f00f5bb0`, `paper-evidence-consistency-audit-6cb9c016`, `paper-quality-coherence-30e6796a`) identified and fixed systematic inconsistencies across configuration, paths, specs, macros, and dashboard setup. The project moved from split-brain state (v1.4.11.2 in workflow, v1.4.6 in paper) to unified infrastructure with centralized config derivation, snakemake.input.* path discipline, dashboard kind/artifact conventions, and spec-documents-code philosophy. This fiber consolidates the audit findings, fixes applied, and operational patterns established.

## Audit Scope and Findings

A parallel 5-agent audit (COSEBIS, harmonic, pure E/B, covariance, paper) found and fixed:

**Configuration split-brain**: fiducial set to v1.4.11.2 in `workflow/config/config.yaml` but paper assumed v1.4.6. Fixed by reverting fiducial to v1.4.6 (size cut 1) with synchronized keys `fiducial.version` and `plotting.fiducial_for_comparison`.

**Code bugs** (4):
- COSEBIS covariance paths hardcoded to v1.4.6 instead of deriving from config
- PTE substring matching failed (bare `"SP_v1.4.5" in path` matched `SP_v1.4.5_leak_corr`). Fixed: filter to `_leak_corr` versions first, sort by length descending
- cl_version_comparison.py iterated all 8 versions instead of 4-item `VERSIONS_LEAK_CORR` subset, causing zip mismatch
- Pure E/B covariance plot shading inverted direction (shaded outside fiducial range instead of inside)

**Hardcoded paths** (3):
- Matplotlib style paths hardcoded in plot_pure_eb_covariance.py, bb_covariance_blind_independence.py. Fixed: use `PAPER_MPLSTYLE` import from plotting_utils.py

**Outdated specs** (5):
- pure_eb_covariance.md spec said "icefire colormap" but code used "vlag"
- cl_data_vector.md, cosebis_data_vector.md, config_space_pte_matrices.md, harmonic_space_pte_matrices.md needed version list and blinds updates
- Spec philosophy clarified: document actual code behavior, not aspirational state

**Stale artifacts and dead code**:
- 3 claim directories with old figures
- 9 PNG files orphaned after claim refactors
- 80 chunk files from old pipeline
- v1.4.10.1 dead code removed (dead for months; mapping to v1.4.6 retained in resolve_covariance_version for backward compatibility)
- Evidence provenance bug fixed (duplicate nmodes in fixture)

**Remaining deferred**:
- Hartlap correction inconsistency between data_vector PTEs and PTE matrices (<1% impact)
- v1.4.11.2 mask area question (separate fiber `mask-methodology-revision-dc4b1bbd`)

## Infrastructure Fixes Applied

### Configuration Management

**Fiducial and version filtering** (`simplify-snakemake-workflow-d6f7f73b`, `derive-version-lists-from-config-f84c2ea6`):
- Added `FIDUCIAL` shorthand in Snakefile: `FIDUCIAL = config['fiducial']` for cleaner access
- Centralized covariance path construction: single `covariance_path()` function in Snakefile
- Derived BASE_VERSIONS and PSEUDO_CL_VERSIONS from `config['versions']` at top level
- VERSION_LABELS dict moved to config.yaml (from 3 hardcoded locations)
- v1.4.10.1 → v1.4.6 mapping via `resolve_covariance_version()` (centralized, 3 previous locations)
- Net result: -56 lines, single source of truth for all version lists

**Blind management** (`centralized-blinds-7d08e64b`):
- Added `blinds: ["A"]` to config.yaml
- All plot rules updated to use `config['fiducial']['blind']` instead of hardcoded `blind="A"`
- Rules updated: cosebis_version_comparison, cosebis_data_vector, config_space_pte_matrices, harmonic_space_pte_matrices, pure_eb_covariance, pure_eb_data_vector
- covariance_blind_consistency intentionally kept with explicit A/B/C for comparison
- Global blind change: edit `fiducial.blind` in config.yaml

**Path discipline** (`fix-version-comparison-scripts-314e9aa1`):
- Requirement: always use `snakemake.input.*` for file paths in scripts, never reconstruct paths independently
- Rules provide versioned inputs (VERSIONS_LEAK_CORR list) — scripts iterate those
- Pattern: zip(snakemake.input.versions, snakemake.input.data) ensures alignment with DAG
- Violators: cosebis_version_comparison.py, cl_version_comparison.py. Fixed and tested.

### Plotting and Paths

**matplotlib style centralization** (`fix-hardcoded-mplstyle-paths-in-fc7805f6`):
- Import `PAPER_MPLSTYLE` from plotting_utils.py (single source: `/automnt/n17data/cdaley/unions/pure_eb/workflow/scripts/plotting_utils.py`)
- Replaced hardcoded `/n17data/cdaley/unions/pure_eb/.../paper.mplstyle` in all plotting scripts
- Added pure_eb_data_vector to localrules (was the only major plotting rule missing)

### Dashboard Architecture

**Artifact key convention** (`dashboard-artifact-key-9d94aa17`):
- Problem: evidence.json artifact keys used `.replace('.', '_')` (e.g., `figure_v1_4_6`), dashboard globbed *.png by stem (e.g., `figure_v1.4.6`), producing duplicates
- Fix: drop dot-to-underscore replacement in evidence.json artifact generation
- Applied to: cosebis_data_vector.py, pure_eb_data_vector.py, cl_data_vector.py

**Fiber and claim visibility** (`dashboard-requires-kind-claim-703ac5a6`):
- Dashboard uses `rule:` tags to map fibers to nodes — the old `kind:` frontmatter field has been removed
- Tag with `rule:<snakemake_rule_name>` to map to results directory

**PTE table structure** (paper-evidence-consistency-audit-6cb9c016):
- Redesigned tables with 3 visually separated groups:
  - COSEBIS: n≤6 (fiducial) + n≤20 (diagnostic)
  - Pure E/B: ξ+^B + ξ-^B + ξ_tot^B
  - Pseudo-Cℓ: C_ℓ^BB
- Macro generation: each statistic group tagged independently in evidence.json

## Specs and Philosophy

**Spec-describes-code principle** (`spec-describes-code-behavior-3b590830`):
- Specs document actual code behavior, not aspirational or planned state
- When code and spec diverge: determine which is correct (often code is fine, spec is stale)
- Example: pure_eb_covariance.md spec said "icefire colormap" but code used "vlag" → updated spec to "vlag"
- Consequence: don't edit specs to prescribe behavior changes; implement code changes, then update specs to reflect them

**Updated specs** (5 consolidated, all now matching code behavior):
1. pure_eb_covariance.md — colormap, version handling
2. cosebis_data_vector.md — version filtering, blind configuration
3. cl_data_vector.md — version filtering, leak_corr subset
4. config_space_pte_matrices.md — version substring matching (length-descending sort + leak_corr filter first)
5. harmonic_space_pte_matrices.md — blind-aware PTE matrix generation

## Paper Quality Audits

**Paper–evidence consistency** (`paper-evidence-consistency-audit-6cb9c016`):
- 8 mechanical checks (COSEBIS mode consistency, macro integrity, figure–claim alignment, PTE table consistency, prose–evidence agreement, spec–paper alignment, citation completeness, config consistency)
- All 8 pass
- v1.4.11.2 ξ-^B PTE corrected (commit 31057e0)
- Covariance cosmology fixed to Planck18 (commit bfb767a)
- PTE tables redesigned with 3-group structure (commits 78f0db4, 6a7cff5)

**Paper quality** (`paper-quality-coherence-30e6796a`, 19 iterations):
- Grounded all hardcoded values in evidence (no fabricated factors)
- Corrected 10' feature amplitudes (1.86–3.40σ, not "1.5σ")
- Fixed 3' spike characterization (v1.4.6-only noise, not "2σ below 5'")
- Corrected v1.4.11.2 harmonic characterization
- Fixed structural contradictions (COSEBIS pass attribution, covariance cosmology, MegaCam FOV vs CCD scale)
- Sharpened Discussion (complementary failure trading, cross-paper evidence)
- Improved precision (removed evaluative language, qualified comparisons)
- 32 labels, 47+ references, 10 figures, 57 macros all verified
- Final commit: 72f4cd3

## Operational Patterns

**Version filtering pattern**:
```python
# Rule provides VERSIONS_LEAK_CORR input (4 items only)
expand("{version}", version=config['versions'], allow_missing=True),
  →  filtered via VERSIONS_LEAK_CORR in Snakefile wildcard constraints

# Script iterates correct subset
for version in snakemake.input.versions:
    # do something
```

**Covariance path pattern**:
```python
# Single Snakefile function
def covariance_path(version):
    """Return covariance file path, mapping v1.4.10.1 to v1.4.6."""
    v = resolve_covariance_version(version)
    return f"path/to/{v}/cov/..."
```

**Dashboard metadata pattern**:
```python
# In Snakemake rule
evidence = {
    "artifact_key": "figure_v1.4.6",  # NO .replace('.', '_')
    "files": [path]
}

# Fiber metadata
```
tags:
    - doc
    - rule:config_space_pte_matrices
    - [other tags]
```
```

**Snakemake path discipline**:
```python
# BAD (path reconstruction, breaks dependency tracking)
data_file = f"results/claims/{rule}/{version}.pkl"

# GOOD (uses DAG-tracked inputs)
data_file = snakemake.input.data[0]
```

## Files Modified (Audit Summary)

**Configuration**:
- `workflow/config/config.yaml` — reverted fiducial to v1.4.6, added `blinds`, `VERSION_LABELS`, centralized version lists

**Snakemake**:
- `workflow/Snakefile` — added FIDUCIAL, covariance_path(), resolve_covariance_version(), version list derivation
- `workflow/rules/*.smk` — removed hardcoded blinds, path construction; use config['fiducial']['blind']

**Scripts** (plotting and data):
- `workflow/scripts/plotting_utils.py` — PAPER_MPLSTYLE exported
- `workflow/scripts/plot_pure_eb_covariance.py` — removed hardcoded mplstyle path, added to localrules
- `workflow/scripts/bb_covariance_blind_independence.py` — removed hardcoded mplstyle path
- `workflow/scripts/cosebis_version_comparison.py` — use snakemake.input.*, filter to leak_corr
- `workflow/scripts/cl_version_comparison.py` — use snakemake.input.*, filter to leak_corr
- `workflow/scripts/cosebis_data_vector.py` — use `config['fiducial']['blind']`, drop artifact key replacement
- `workflow/scripts/pure_eb_data_vector.py` — use `config['fiducial']['blind']`, drop artifact key replacement
- `workflow/scripts/cl_data_vector.py` — use `config['fiducial']['blind']`, drop artifact key replacement
- `workflow/scripts/config_space_pte_matrices.py` — filter to leak_corr, fix substring matching (length sort)

**Specs**:
- `workflow/config/pure_eb_covariance.md` — colormap, version handling
- `workflow/config/cosebis_data_vector.md` — version filtering
- `workflow/config/cl_data_vector.md` — version filtering
- `workflow/config/config_space_pte_matrices.md` — version substring matching
- `workflow/config/harmonic_space_pte_matrices.md` — blind handling

**Paper**:
- `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/unions_bmodes.tex` — hardcoded PTEs → macros, "three" → "four" versions
- `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/claims_macros.tex` — expanded to ~57 macros
- `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/pte_table_results.tex` — redesigned 3-group structure

## Next Steps After Audit

1. Regenerate all claims: `snakemake all_claims paper_macros --forcerun -j 100`
2. Verify dashboard: all data vector claims show 9 figures (not 18), all spec fibers visible
3. Paper PDF check: all macros resolve, no red placeholders
4. Manual inspection: any orphaned artifacts in results/ directories

fibers.title: list
Catalog versions: v1.4.5 through v1.4.11.3 integration history
fibers.body:
## Lede

The B-modes paper integrates four catalog versions across a progression of improvements: **v1.4.5** (Initial, fails harmonic-space), **v1.4.6** (Fiducial, passes all tests, size cuts enforced), **v1.4.8** (Masking improvements, fails config-space due to additional systematic), **v1.4.11.3** (Relaxed flags via SExtractor refinement, replaces v1.4.11.2 PSF size fix). The fiducial choice is **v1.4.6** — it's the first version passing both config-space and harmonic-space PTE tests simultaneously. **v1.4.11.3** supersedes v1.4.11.2 after a PSF size bug fix; the two are identical in selection (78M objects, identical n_eff and σ_e) but v1.4.11.3's leak-corrected ellipticities differ due to corrected PSF model. Each version has covariance computed from its own mask and survey geometry; v1.4.11.2 and v1.4.11.3 share covariance structure (n(z) fallback to v1.4.6 pending full generation).

## Version Map

| Version | Label | Role | Config Entry | Objects | Covariance | Mask | Notes |
|---------|-------|------|--------------|---------|-----------|------|-------|
| v1.4.5 | Initial | baseline; fails harmonic | SP_v1.4.5_leak_corr | 59M | own | ~2200 deg² | First candidate; harmonic PTE 0.034 (fails) |
| v1.4.6 | Size cuts | **fiducial** | SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr | 67M | own | 2405 deg² | Config PTE 0.37/0.24/0.72, Harmonic 0.35 (passes both) |
| v1.4.8 | Masking | comparison; fails config | SP_v1.4.8_leak_corr | 73M | own | ~2350 deg² | Additional B-mode systematic beyond PSF leakage |
| v1.4.11.2 | Blends | developmental; PTE regression | SP_v1.4.11.2_leak_corr | 78M | own (2504 deg²) | 2504 deg² (superset of v1.4.6) | Relaxed SExtractor flags; ξ₋^B fails (0.030) with own cov; passes (0.17) with v1.4.6 cov |
| v1.4.11.3 | Relaxed flags | **current** | SP_v1.4.11.3_leak_corr | 78M | v1.4.11.2 | v1.4.11.2 | PSF size fix only; same selection as v1.4.11.2 |

## Key Decisions

**Fiducial choice (v1.4.6):** Established via comprehensive testing across two statistical spaces (config and harmonic). v1.4.5 fails harmonic-space; v1.4.8 fails config-space (indicating systematics beyond PSF leakage). v1.4.6 passes both — the necessary and sufficient criterion. Decision finalized in `feb-2026-project-audit` and `b-modes-paper-completion`.

**v1.4.10.1 removal:** Dead code path. Initially considered (notation: "10 = sample number, .1 = PSF patch") but superseded by v1.4.11.2 integration. Code references remain in Snakefile (`resolve_covariance_version()` mapping) for DAG resolution only; version removed from active config list during Feb 2026 audit.

**v1.4.11.2 → v1.4.11.3 transition:** Nomenclature change (Blends → Relaxed flags) reflects SExtractor methodology clarification. v1.4.11.2 was developmental label; v1.4.11.3 is production (PSF size fix). Catalog semantics identical (78,188,716 objects, n_eff=7.232, σ_e=0.381); only leak-corrected ellipticities differ.

**Leak-corrected only for v1.4.5+:** Comparison also generates non-leak-corrected variants (SP_v1.4.5, SP_v1.4.6, SP_v1.4.8 without `_leak_corr` suffix) to quantify correction impact. Full angular range shows 1.3–3.7× improvement (pure-mode), 1.9–2.5× (harmonic), 2.8–6.6× (COSEBIS); fiducial scale cuts already exclude contaminated scales, reducing benefit to 1–1.5×.

## Technical Foundations

### Version String Matching

**Substring collision problem:** Config contains versions like `SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr` and `SP_v1.4.5_leak_corr`. Naive substring match `"SP_v1.4.5" in path` matches both `SP_v1.4.5_leak_corr` AND `SP_v1.4.5*` variants. **Solution:** Sort versions by length descending before matching, so longer (more specific) versions match first. When only leak-corrected variants exist in data directory, filter to `_leak_corr` versions first.

**Implementation:** `config_space_pte_matrices.py` and related scripts apply length-descending sort on version list and explicit `_leak_corr` filtering (see fiber `config-space-pte-matrices-ver-460e81cd`).

### Covariance Version Mapping

**`resolve_covariance_version()` function** (Snakefile):
- v1.4.5, v1.4.6, v1.4.8, v1.4.11.2 → own covariance (each has dedicated CosmoCov run)
- v1.4.11.3 → v1.4.11.2 (identical selection, same n_eff/σ_e, covariance structure valid)
- v1.4.10.1 → v1.4.6 (legacy fallback; version removed from config, rule retained for DAG)
- non-leak-corr variants → normalize to `_leak_corr` path for covariance lookup

**N(z) fallback:** v1.4.11.3 uses v1.4.6 n(z) via `build_redshift_path()` pending full generation for v1.4.11.3. All covariance rules import directly from `sp_validation.cosmology.PLANCK18` — no version-specific cosmology in config.

### Leak-Correction Architecture

**Catalog structure:** All v1.4.x catalogs (FITS files) contain both uncorrected (`e1`, `e2`) and leak-corrected (`e1_leak_corrected`, `e2_leak_corrected`) ellipticity columns. **No separate data files.** CosmologyValidation's `cat_config.yaml` switches column usage via `e1_col` (uncorrected) vs `e1_col_corrected` (corrected). For version "SP_v1.4.6", the pipeline reads from uncorrected columns; for "SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr", same FITS file, corrected columns.

**Config path normalization:** When computing covariance for non-leak-corr version, pipeline normalizes path to `_leak_corr` variant — covariance survey properties (n_eff, σ_e, footprint) are identical regardless of which columns are used for correlation functions. Decision documented in `covariance-uses-leak-corr-path-c943d523` fiber.

## Mask Story

### v1.4.11.2 Integration and PTE Regression

v1.4.11.2 introduced a mask as strict superset of v1.4.6: +99 deg² (~4%) of boundary infill from deblending processing (2405 → 2504 deg²). Full covariance computed (Lisa's CosmoCov run, mask Cls at nside=8192). Expected minor differences; instead:

- **Pure E/B ξ₋^B:** Passes (PTE 0.17) with v1.4.6 covariance; **fails (PTE 0.030)** with v1.4.11.2 own covariance
- **Joint PTE:** Passes (0.16) → fails (0.012)

**Root cause (not noise power):** Mask's angular power spectrum differs significantly. v1.4.11.2 mask Cls show +8% power at large scales (ℓ<100), -17% at small scales (ℓ>5000). Despite larger area (+99 deg²), noise power σ_e²/(A×n_eff) is **identical** — n_eff drops exactly enough (7.53 → 7.23 arcmin⁻²) to offset area increase. The tightened covariance from redder mask Cls is sole mechanism pushing ξ₋^B below PTE threshold.

**Implication:** Mask methodology choices (nside resolution, selection cuts applied) directly impact covariance off-diagonal structure and downstream statistical inference. Fiber `mask-methodology-revision-dc4b1bbd` documents ongoing discussion about computing mask from comprehensive catalog with only n_exp > 3 cut, potentially changing v1.4.6 area from 2405 → ~2834 deg² and requiring all covariances to be recomputed.

### v1.4.11.3 Mask Reuse

v1.4.11.3 uses v1.4.11.2 covariance (and mask) directly — both computed during v1.4.11.2 integration. PSF size fix doesn't alter galaxy selection, weights, or footprint geometry. Mask Cls are valid; only lease-corrected ellipticities differ.

## Configuration Sync Points

**Fiducial setting (config.yaml):**
```yaml
fiducial:
  version: SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr

plotting:
  fiducial_for_comparison: "SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr"
  version_labels:
    SP_v1.4.5_leak_corr: "Initial"
    SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr: "Size cuts"
    SP_v1.4.8_leak_corr: "Masking"
    SP_v1.4.11.3_leak_corr: "Relaxed flags"
```

**Both keys must stay in sync** — paper text, generated macros (VERSION_WORDS in `generate_paper_macros.py`), and PTE tables all derive from `fiducial.version`. Macro generation uses word forms (`"11.3": "ElevenThree"`) to avoid latex conflicts.

**Paper version references:** Reduced to 2 occurrences per version (data section intro + Table 1 only). Everywhere else uses labels (Initial, Size cuts, Masking, Relaxed flags) for brevity and consistency.

## Paper Integration

**Four versions appear in publication:**
- Table 1 (data section): All four versions with labels and fiducial designation
- Results section (lines 438–462): Narrative explaining PTE comparisons and fiducial choice
- Figure captions: Version labels (no version numbers) for dashboard versions; fiducial version unadorned
- Discussion (line 492): Comparison of leakage correction impact
- Conclusions (line 509): Fiducial stability across methodological variants

**Figure workflow:** Claims rules (`pure_eb_data_vector`, `cosebis_data_vector`, `cl_data_vector`) produce exactly **9 figures each** via `iter_version_figures()` helper:
- 1 paper figure (fiducial, leak-corrected, no title)
- 8 dashboard figures (4 versions × uncorrected/corrected with labels)

Auto-copied to `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/Figures/` via `paper_figure_*` outputs. Figures regenerate only when upstream claims change (not automatic with plotting utility updates due to snakemake import tracking limitation — requires `--forcerun` for utils changes).

## Known Issues and Decisions

**Mask methodology under discussion:** Current approach (apply all selection cuts before masking at nside=8192) may underestimate footprint area. Comprehensive catalog approach could change v1.4.6 area from 2405 → ~2834 deg², cascading into covariance regeneration for all versions. Deferred pending Lisa/Martin alignment (fiber `mask-methodology-revision-dc4b1bbd`, active).
