Text references it where COSEBIS PTEs are quoted. Paper still 13 pages — figure floats to page 9 between Discussion and Conclusions.

fibers.title: Ralph spec: conducting-research skill must activate first
fibers.outcome: Ralph loops that involve research claims and paper writing need /conducting-research activated FIRST during survey phase, before any code changes. Without it, iterations drift into ad-hoc edits instead of structured claim-evidence work. Spec skills section should be prescriptive about order: (1) /conducting-research, (2) /snakemake before snakemake commands, (3) /writing before paper edits. Dropped /implementing-code — redundant when research + snakemake cover the workflow.

fibers.title: Douglas Scott comments round 2 (L112–L622)
fibers.body:
# Spec

You are in a Ralph loop — autonomous iterative contribution toward a desired state.

## Skills

Activate before beginning:

- /data-visualization
- /writing
- /ralph

## Rhythm

1. **Survey** — `felt downstream douglas-scott-comments-round-2-94140f4c`, check which subfibers remain open. Group them by what can be done together.
2. **Batch the trivial** — Simple text edits (wording swaps, deletions, find/replace) can be handled in parallel via subagents or sequentially in a single pass. Don't spend a whole iteration on one line change.
3. **Focus on the substantial** — Prose rewrites and figure changes deserve individual attention. Read context before editing prose. Dry-run before regenerating figures.
4. **Notice** — As you work, file new subfibers for anything you notice. Leave things better than you found them.
5. **Exit** — Always `kill $PPID`.

## Exit Rules

**Made ANY contribution:** `kill $PPID`. Do NOT close the parent fiber.

**Made ZERO contributions AND nothing left:** `felt off douglas-scott-comments-round-2-94140f4c -r "All comments addressed"`, then `kill $PPID`.

---

## Goal

Address all Douglas Scott round 2 comments — paper text (L112–L622) and claims dashboard figure feedback — and leave the paper better than you found it.

## Design

33 subfibers in three categories:

### Text edits (quick)
Simple wording changes, deletions, find/replace. Grep first for systematic changes.
- L112 wording, L118 roughly→approximately, L127 delete Paper IV, L198 comprises, L224 ≲1→better than, L224 per~cent
- Systematic: Euclid italics, ShapePipe \texttt, table \noalign spacing, range dash notation (θ = 0–500 not [0, 500]), arcsecond A&A notation
- L258 modes→mode number (check consistency, decide)

### Prose rewrites (medium)
Read surrounding context before editing. Keep the paper's voice.
- L115: merge mini paragraph into preceding B-mode statistics paragraph
- L175: table 1 caption — proper sentences, essential info only
- L199: explain metacalibration-corrected
- L219: rewrite multi-statistic rationale — be substantive about *why* all stats must pass (different sensitivity to masking/binning), not vague "systematic contamination"
- L320: verify SSC explanation from Jan round survived
- Weave plot interpretation into narrative throughout Results — every figure cite should form part of the argument
- L622: Planck bib — only "Planck Collaboration" as author
- L144: explain LSST
- L408: PTE table spacing + consider single-column (flip rows/columns?)

### Figure/plot changes (heavy)
These require modifying plotting scripts and regenerating via snakemake. Use `snakemake <target> --dry-run` first, then run.
- **Font sizes**: establish a global system (mplstyle or per-script) so all figures match body text at their column width (single or double). This is the umbrella — touches every figure.
- **Alpha**: increase for secondary data series (E/ambig) across all plots — too faint printed.
- **Version comparison boxes** (all 3 stats): wider boxes, black outlines above data, check husl colorblind safety, per-stat tuning (COSEBIS: more x-offset; pure E/B: less x-offset, thinner, shared y-axes, legend top-left on upper-right)
- **Cl data vector**: delete "slight x-shift" text; distinct harmonic-space color scheme
- **Config PTE**: subplot titles bottom-right in empty triangle; colorbar height = subplot height
- **Pure E/B data vector**: subplot titles inside (top-left)
- **Harmonic-config COSEBIS**: legend to upper center
- **Covariance figure**: x-label tick alignment (may already be fixed)
- **L429 figure**: factor out 10^{-7} in y-label

### Ordering guidance

Each iteration: pick at least one heavy item (figure/plot change) to work on alongside trivial text edits and prose rewrites. Tackle the font size umbrella early since it informs all subsequent figure work. **Snakemake: dry-run only until ALL script, text, and plotting changes across ALL subfibers are complete.** Then one final snakemake run to regenerate everything. Don't burn cluster time iterating on figures one at a time.

## Context

Paper: `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/unions_bmodes.tex`
Build: `cd docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes && app latexmk -pdf -output-directory=build unions_bmodes.tex`
Plotting scripts: `workflow/scripts/` (plot_eb.py, plot_cosebis.py, etc.)
Style: `workflow/scripts/plotting_utils.py` — `PAPER_MPLSTYLE`, `iter_version_figures()`, `make_pte_colormap()`
Config: `workflow/config/config.yaml`
Figures land in: `docs/unions_release/unions_bmodes/Figures/`

For plot changes: modify script → `snakemake <rule> --dry-run` → run → verify figure → close fiber.

## Verification

After figures are regenerated, **read each updated plot** (use the Read tool on PNGs) and verify it actually addresses the original comment or request. For each figure change:

1. Re-read the subfiber's concern (or Douglas's original comment) to recall what was asked
2. Look at the regenerated figure
3. Ask the user (via `AskUserQuestion`) whether the change addresses the feedback — batch up to 4 figures per question round

Don't assume script changes produced the right visual result. Eyes on every plot.

## Completion

All subfibers closed (including any you add). Paper builds without error. Figures regenerated, visually verified, and confirmed to address the original comments.

## Comments
**2026-02-09 03:15** — Added huff.mandelbaum17 citation via managing-bibliography skill. Fetched exact ADS BibTeX entry (arXiv:1702.02600, 2017). Updated unions_bmodes.bib with full abstract and metadata.
**2026-02-09 03:31** — All 33 subfibers closed. Script changes complete: font system (10pt base, FIG_WIDTH_SINGLE=3.54), alpha, subplot titles, box plots, colorbar, E_n×10^10, harmonic color scheme. Next: snakemake dry-run to identify which claim rules need regeneration, then final run.
**2026-02-09 04:39** — Iteration 4: Fixed bracket range notation in COSEBIS figure legends (θ ∈ [a,b]' → θ = a–b') across 3 scripts/4 occurrences. Fixed grammar (L271: 'correspondingly angular' → 'correspondingly finer angular'). Fixed abstract (L71: ξ+ → ξ+^B). Regenerated all affected claims (7 jobs). Verified all figures visually. Paper builds clean (14p).
fibers.outcome: All 44 subfibers closed across 5 iterations. Text edits: L112 wording, L118 roughly→approximately, L127 Paper IV expanded, L144 LSST defined, L146 arcsecond notation, L198 comprises→is composed of, L199 metacalibration explained (huff.mandelbaum17 added), L219 multi-statistic rationale rewritten, L224 per cent fixed, L258 mode number, L271 correspondingly finer, L320 SSC verified, L622 Planck bib. Systematic: Euclid italics, ShapePipe texttt, noalign table spacing, range dash notation, percent→per cent consistency. Prose: L115 pseudo-Cl merged, L175 Table 1 caption, plot interpretation woven into Results, weave plot interpretation strengthened. Figure changes: 10pt global font system (FIG_WIDTH_SINGLE=3.54), alpha=0.45 for secondary series, subplot titles inside (pure E/B top-left, config PTE bottom-right), version comparison boxes (wider, black outlines, point-estimate limits), Cl distinct harmonic color scheme (dark blue/burnt orange), slight-x-shift text deleted, colorbar height matched, E_n×10^10 factor, COSEBIS x-offset widened, pure E/B x-offset tightened + shared y-axes, bracket→dash range notation in figure legends, harmonic-config COSEBIS legend upper center. All claims regenerated via snakemake. Paper builds clean (14 pages). All figures visually verified against original comments.

fibers.title: Mask Cls angular structure drives v1.4.11.2 PTE regression
fibers.outcome: v1.4.11.2 pure E/B ξ± went from passing to failing at fiducial cuts when we switched from v1.4.6 covariance to v1.4.11.2's own covariance. Root cause: mask angular structure, not noise power. The v1.4.11.2 mask (deblended boundary infill) has +8% more large-scale power (ℓ<100) and -17% less small-scale power (ℓ>5000) vs v1.4.6. Noise power σ_e²/(A×n_eff) is identical between masks — n_eff drops by exactly the area increase (7.53→7.23 arcmin⁻²). Off-diagonal covariance from mask Cls is the sole mechanism. Verified by recomputing v1.4.11.2 PTEs with v1.4.6 covariance: ξ-^B PTE goes from 0.030 (fail) back to 0.17 (pass), joint PTE from 0.012 to 0.16. Plot: results/mask_cls_ratio.png.

Values like 2.37e-06 now render as proper 2.37×10⁻⁶. Bold/italic nesting works: \textbf{\num{0.006}}. Also changed PTE table bold threshold from 0.01 to 0.05, removed italic tier. fibers.title: Pure E/B pipeline: monolithic to blind-aware chunks
fibers.body:
# Pure E/B Pipeline: Monolithic to Blind-Aware Chunks

## Lede

The pure E/B analysis pipeline was refactored from a monolithic 60-minute, 32GB-per-blind `precompute_pure_eb_blind` rule to a lean scatter-gather architecture where the chunk pipeline carries the blind wildcard throughout. Blind A is now a regular blind (no special base case), intermediate files carry the `{version}_{blind}_{chunk_id}` signature, and seeding is consistent across blinds: each chunk uses seed `42 + chunk_id` to ensure reproducibility while preserving blind independence. The monolithic rule and its companion covariance script were eliminated, and all downstream consumers updated to request blind A explicitly.

## Before: The Monolithic Design

The original pipeline precomputed pure E/B statistics in a single, heavyweight rule:

```
precompute_pure_eb_blind({version}, {blind})
  → 60 minutes, 32 GB RAM per blind
  → outputs: pickled binning matrix, statistical summary
```

This worked but was inefficient:
- **Resource overhead**: Each blind ran a full 60-minute compute job, even though most of the work (covariance matrix computation) was identical across blinds
- **Base file semantics**: Blind A was special—it served as the default when no blind was specified, creating implicit dependencies
- **Covariance rule**: A separate monolithic `precompute_pure_eb_covariance.py` script repeated work and complicated the DAG
- **Blind independence testing**: Proving that results were independent of blind required explicit equality checks; the pipeline didn't enforce it structurally

## After: Blind-Aware Chunks

The refactored pipeline applies the same scatter-gather pattern used elsewhere:

```
precompute_pure_eb_chunk({version}, {blind}, {chunk_id})
  → 5-10 minutes per chunk
  → outputs: {version}_{blind}_{chunk_id}_eb.pkl

gather_pure_eb_chunks({version}, {blind})
  → collects all chunks for one (version, blind) pair
  → outputs: {version}_{blind}_eb.pkl
```

### Key Changes

1. **Blind wildcard throughout**: Every intermediate file carries `{blind}` in its name:
   - Old: `SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr_chunk_0.pkl` (implicitly blind A)
   - New: `SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr_A_chunk_0.pkl`, `...B_chunk_0.pkl`, etc.

2. **Blind A is a regular blind**: No base case. Consumers must explicitly request `_A_`:
   ```python
   expand("SP_v1.4.6_leak_corr_{blind}_eb.pkl", blind=["A", "B", "C"])
   ```
   This makes blind independence structural rather than tested.

3. **Seeding strategy**: All blinds use the same seed formula `42 + chunk_id`:
   - Different blinds → different N(z) inputs → different covariance draws
   - Same chunk_id across blinds → same random number sequence given to the uniform RNG
   - Result: chunk-level consistency without blind coupling

4. **Deleted redundant code**:
   - `precompute_pure_eb_blind` rule (monolithic)
   - `precompute_pure_eb_covariance.py` script
   - All special-casing for blind A in downstream rules

5. **DRY'd via wildcard constraints**: Single scatter-gather rule pair with optional blind parameter via `wildcard_constraints` regex, eliminating parallel rule copies.

## Architectural Benefits

### Performance
- **Chunking**: 60-min monolithic job → 5-10 min chunks → parallelizable across cluster
- **Resource efficiency**: No GPU overhead; pure CPU, scales with available cores

### Clarity
- **Blind handling is explicit**: No implicit defaults; dependencies are transparent
- **Reproducibility**: Seed strategy guarantees bit-for-bit reproducibility across runs and blinds
- **Debugging**: Failures are localized to specific (version, blind, chunk) tuples

### Robustness
- **Blind independence by design**: The pipeline structure enforces it; can't accidentally use blind A as a fallback
- **Covariance consistency**: All blinds compute their own covariance from their own N(z)—no sharing of base statistics

## Resolved Issues

**Audit finding I-3** (binning matrix consistency): The monolithic rule could silently use old intermediate binning matrices when re-running on a subset of blinds. The chunk pipeline forces regeneration per blind, preventing silent staleness.

## Decisions Recorded

Decisions documented in supporting fibers:
- `complete-bb-covariance-blind-68a8d055`: Blind independence claim spec and results (BB vs EE variance under MC)
- `fix-mc-seed-in-per-blind-pure-e-f3afb676`: MC seeding strategy for per-blind covariance
- `extend-precompute-pure-eb-chunk-519dd1dc`, `...6e0a9cfd`, `...a78f7b4b`: Incremental chunks extension and blind param rollout

## Implementation Span

February 2026, integrated into the Feb 2 project audit (`feb-2026-project-audit-fiducial-f00f5bb0`).

fibers.title: σ_e convention: per-component in paper, combined for CosmoCov
fibers.outcome: Paper Table 1 quotes per-component σ_e (0.27-0.28). cat_config.yaml feeds CosmoCov the combined value σ_e(combined) = σ_e(per-comp) × √2 ≈ 0.380-0.397. Verified numerically.

fibers.outcome: Dashboard is an explorable analysis tapestry, not just paper figures. Ecut versions are valuable for inspection but should not appear in paper version comparison plots. Four main catalogs (v1.4.5, v1.4.6, v1.4.8, v1.4.11.3) suffice for paper.

fibers.title: Consider non-Gaussian contribution to BB covariance
fibers.body: Current BB covariance is Gaussian-only from NaMaster. Non-Gaussian would increase errors, making null test less stringent. For thorough analysis, could add NG via OneCovariance (same as EE). Low priority since Gaussian-only is conservative.
fibers.outcome: obsolete

fibers.title: Address Douglas Scott comments
fibers.body:
Dec 23 review on UNIONS paper III. Key items: COSEBIs explanation, Fig 3 panel labeling confusion, super-sample covariance explanation, Figs 5-6 contour styling. Plus minor style/grammar/TeX on attached PDF. Leave a child fiber explaining how each comment was addressed, or why not.

Non-PDF commments:
*The Intro says "weak lensing distorts..." and then cites 2000 papers
with data - but if this is a history lesson, then there are earlier
papers discussing the method (Blandford, Miralda-Escude, Kaiser, etc.).
*COSEBIs needs to be explained when first mentioned.
*What's "repeating additive shear bias"?
*Are harmonic space and configuration-space lensing estimates really
"complementary"?  Aren't they just two ways of representing the same
information?
*There's confusion in Fig. 3 - are the panels labelled wrong maybe?  The
top panel definitely *isn't* consistent with zero, while the bottom
panel could well be.  But the caption says that BB is consistent with
zero.  Section 4.3 says that the same figure shows EE and BB.  Are the
panels actually BB and EB?
*Super-sample covariance needs to be explained (briefly).
*I don't think that the contours are helpful in Figs. 5 and 6.  They're
hard to see and look too clunky.  Couldn't you just set the colour table
so that blue corresponds to the threshold you want to highlight?
